Title: Rapid local and systemic jasmonate signalling drives the initiation and establishment of plant systemic immunity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: marks rapid initiation and establishment of systemic acquired resistance and systemic immune signalling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ETI by DCavrRpm1/DCavrRpt2/DCavrRps4; wounding; exogenous JA or coronatine; calcium blocker LaCl3; JA biosynthesis inhibitors
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rapidly induced in petiole, vasculature and epidermal cells of systemic leaves after ETI or wounding
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native JISS1 promoter used for LUC and GFP reporters
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter::LUC reporter imaging; GFP localization; T-DNA loss-of-function mutant; pharmacology; electrophysiology; SAR assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GLR3.3; GLR3.6 (functional relationship for SISP, not direct physical interaction)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: jiss1 mutant abolishes systemic surface electrical potentials but does not impair SAR to Pseudomonas syringae
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JISS1 expression is a sensitive jasmonate-responsive reporter of rapid systemic immune signalling; JISS1 itself is required for SISPs but dispensable for SAR
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray dataset NASCARRAYS-403; single-cell transcriptomics discussed without accession
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JISS1 promoter activity reports very early ETI-induced systemic signalling before visible HR collapse. 2. JISS1 induction requires jasmonate biosynthesis/perception and calcium signalling, and responds to wounding and coronatine/JA. 3. JISS1 protein localizes to the ER in vasculature and epidermis; jiss1 mutants lose systemic electrical potentials but retain SAR, indicating reporter/auxiliary signalling function rather than essential SAR execution.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a dynamic reporter tool to dissect or screen for rapid systemic immunity pathways rather than as a direct target to improve SAR alone.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper’s main experimentally centered gene; previously called A70. Despite being the title-independent focal gene, it is not an enzyme or TF, so classified as Common_Gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: links calcium/electrical systemic signalling to immune signal propagation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: vasculature-associated; discussion cites ER localization in phloem sieve elements
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; electrophysiology; JISS1::LUC crosses; SAR growth assay in double mutant context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: glr mutants abolish SISPs but retain SAR
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for ETI-induced systemic immunity surface potentials but not required for JISS1 reporter activation or SAR to Psm4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. glr3.3a mutants failed to propagate SISPs after ETI. 2. Loss of GLR3.3 did not alter JISS1::LUC systemic reporter dynamics. 3. GLR-dependent electrical signalling is therefore separable from SAR establishment to Pseudomonas syringae.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning long-distance electrical or anti-herbivory signalling, but likely not necessary for bacterial SAR enhancement.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core because directly tested mechanistically in Results; not a pathway enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: links calcium/electrical systemic signalling to immune signal propagation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: vasculature-associated; discussion cites tonoplast membrane of xylem contact cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; electrophysiology; JISS1::LUC crosses; SAR growth assay in double mutant context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: glr mutants abolish SISPs but retain SAR
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for ETI-induced systemic immunity surface potentials but not required for JISS1 reporter activation or SAR to Psm4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. glr3.6a mutants failed to propagate SISPs after ETI. 2. Loss of GLR3.6 did not alter JISS1::LUC systemic reporter dynamics. 3. GLR3.6 contributes to electrical but not SAR-essential signalling in this bacterial system.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially valuable for engineering systemic electrical or wound/herbivory signalling, with caution that bacterial SAR is unaffected.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core because directly assayed in mutant and reporter analyses; not an enzyme or TF.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for initiation and establishment of systemic acquired resistance (SAR) and systemic immunity-associated signalling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: unstable allene oxide precursor of OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts fatty acid hydroperoxides such as 13-HPOT into an allene oxide intermediate in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for JA production in this study
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ETI triggered by Pseudomonas syringae DCavrRpm1; inhibitor treatments phenidone, DIECA, jarin-1; exogenous JA or coronatine rescue
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native mutant allele; no transgene promoter relevant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; reporter assay with JISS1::LUC; SAR growth assay; chemical rescue with exogenous JA or coronatine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of AOS abolishes ETI-induced JISS1::LUC local/systemic signalling and abolishes SAR; exogenous JA or COR restores reporter activity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray/NASCARRAYS-403 re-analysis mentioned for jasmonate-responsive genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The aos jasmonate-biosynthetic mutant failed to induce systemic JISS1::LUC after DCavrRpm1 challenge. 2. aos plants were SAR deficient despite normal local HR timing. 3. Exogenous JA or coronatine restored JISS1::LUC signalling in aos, placing AOS upstream of bioactive jasmonate signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing AOS-mediated jasmonate production could strengthen rapid systemic immunity, but deployment should consider broader JA defence-growth tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOS is a core causal pathway gene because mutant analysis directly linked jasmonate biosynthesis to SAR establishment.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for systemic acquired resistance establishment and jasmonate-responsive systemic gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: hormone receptor F-box protein / jasmonate co-receptor component
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein; SCFCOI1 jasmonate receptor complex component
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential jasmonate perception switch for ETI-induced systemic signalling and SAR
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonoyl-isoleucine; coronatine mimic; ETI triggered by DCavrRpm1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ repressors; JISS1 reporter activation; JAZ10 systemic expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes jasmonate-responsive transcription upon perception of JA-Ile/COR
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: jasmonate signal transduction controlling systemic immune reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ETI by DCavrRpm1; exogenous JA or COR; wound response; electrophysiology/SISP assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: hormone perception leading to ubiquitin-mediated derepression of jasmonate-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: JAZ family negative feedback is discussed; systemic induction of JAZ genes indicates feedback within JA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant coi1-16; JISS1::LUC assay; JAZ10::GUS assay; SAR growth assay; electrophysiology
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: coi1 mutant is noted to be more resistant to local DC challenge than wild type, indicating context-dependent immunity tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of COI1 abolishes JA/COR-induced JISS1 reporter activation, abolishes ETI-induced systemic JISS1::LUC and JAZ10::GUS, abolishes SISPs, and abolishes SAR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1-16 abolished ETI-induced systemic JISS1::LUC and systemic JAZ10::GUS activation. 2. coi1-16 did not respond to exogenous JA or COR with JISS1::LUC induction, confirming defective jasmonate perception. 3. coi1-16 lacked SAR and systemic surface electrical potentials, demonstrating that jasmonate perception is essential for rapid systemic immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: COI1-dependent JA perception is a central target for engineering systemic immunity, but manipulation must account for pleiotropic defence tradeoffs and altered local pathogen susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COI1 is treated as a regulatory core gene because the paper centers on jasmonate perception rather than metabolism alone.
